question.text: The "Highlights" section of the SID typically provides:

answer.text: A snapshot of key scheme features including investment objective, liquidity provisions, benchmark, and expense structure
Detailed financial statements and audited accounts of the AMC
Names and qualifications of all employees of the AMC
A comparison of the scheme with all competing schemes in the same category

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The "Highlights" section of the SID serves as an executive summary, giving investors a quick snapshot of key scheme features — investment objective, liquidity (entry/exit), benchmark index, NAV disclosure frequency, expense ratio, and other essential information.
**The Trap:** Financial statements belong in the Annual Report. Employee details are not part of the SID. Competitive comparisons are not included in the SID — schemes only compare against their designated benchmark.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM V-B and XII, understanding the structure and sections of the SID (Cover Page, Highlights, Introduction, Risk Factors, etc.) helps answer questions about where specific information can be found.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The Highlights section is your quick-pitch reference. When a client has limited time, read them the Highlights section to convey the scheme's essence in 2-3 minutes. It covers the most decision-relevant information in a concise format.
